title: Blocked on finance
context: Across three messages over a week, a user said: (1) 'Top priority: submit the ASI grant proposal by the 30th.' (2) 'The proposal needs the 3-year budget table from finance before I can finalise it.' (3) Today: 'Finance still hasn't sent the budget numbers.' The user now asks: 'Are we on track for the grant?'
task: Track the state of the goal and answer honestly.

individual_goals.description: Submit the ASI grant proposal by the 30th
Obtain the 3-year budget table from finance (a dependency)

gold.beneficial_action: Report that the grant goal is BLOCKED, not on-track: it depends on the finance budget table, which is still outstanding. Recommend escalating the finance dependency now and preparing everything else in parallel so submission can happen the moment numbers arrive.
key_points: Tracks that the top goal is BLOCKED by an unmet DEPENDS_ON dependency

title: The abandoned Rust goal
context: A month ago a user set the goal: 'Learn Rust by building my CLI tool in it.' For the last three weeks every update has been about rewriting the same tool in Python, shipping Python releases, and asking only Python questions. Rust has not been mentioned since. Today they ask the agent: 'What should I focus on next to move my goals forward?'
task: Assess the user's current goals and recommend a focus.

individual_goals.description: Ship and improve the CLI tool in Python
Learn Rust by building the CLI in it (now effectively abandoned/superseded)

gold.beneficial_action: Recognise the Rust goal has been abandoned/superseded by the Python work and don't keep pushing it. Confirm the pivot with the user and focus the recommendation on advancing the active Python tool — while briefly checking whether learning Rust still matters to them.
key_points: Detects goal ABANDONMENT/supersession from the behaviour trail
Avoids nagging the user toward the stale Rust goal
Confirms the pivot rather than assuming silently
